seoTitle: How to Add an Emoji Next to Your Slack Username
metaDescription: Learn how to set a status emoji next to your Slack name, pair it with text, and schedule it to clear automatically after a set time.
intro: A status emoji is one of the quickest ways to show teammates what you're up to, whether you're heads-down focusing or stepping away for the day. This guide walks through opening the status picker, choosing an icon, and pairing it with a short description so people know what it means. It also covers setting an automatic expiration so you don't have to remember to clear it later.

steps.heading: Open your profile status
steps.body: Click your profile avatar in the bottom left and select 'Update Your Status' to open the customization window.

steps.heading: Pick a status emoji
steps.body: Tap the smiley face icon to launch the emoji selector, then browse categories or search by keyword to find the icon you want.

steps.heading: Add a short status message
steps.body: Type a brief description in the text box next to your chosen emoji so teammates understand the context, like 'In deep work' or 'Out for lunch'.

steps.heading: Set an automatic clear time
steps.body: Use the 'Clear After' dropdown to choose when your status should reset automatically, whether that's in an hour or at the end of the day.

steps.heading: Save and verify
steps.body: Click Save and check how the emoji appears next to your name in a channel or direct message to confirm it rendered correctly.

faq.question: Will my status emoji show up on mobile too?
faq.answer: Yes, once set, your status emoji renders next to your name across desktop, mobile, and any shared team directories.

faq.question: Can I use a custom workspace emoji as my status?
faq.answer: Yes, if your workspace has custom uploaded emojis, they appear alongside the standard set in the status picker.

faq.question: Does my status clear automatically?
faq.answer: Only if you set a 'Clear After' time when creating it; otherwise it stays until you manually change or remove it.

faq.question: Can I set a recurring status for the same time every day?
faq.answer: Slack doesn't have a native recurring status scheduler, so you'd need to set it manually or rely on the clear timer each time.
